metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact `TicketStateOut` field declaration for `attestations` (type annotation and default) and clarify whether 'omitted when empty' means `None` or field-absent, so the MCP outputSchema advertises the correct shape.
findings.finding: The plan requires adding `attestations` to the MCP ticket-state response (`TicketStateOut` in `mcp_server.py`), but neither the plan body nor any AC specifies the Pydantic field type to declare. The current `TicketStateOut` has `extra='allow'` so the field passes through implicitly — but the plan does not state whether an explicit typed field should be added, and if so, what its type annotation should be (`dict | None`, `dict[str, dict] | None`, or left as passthrough).
findings.location: Scope: 'src/rebar/mcp_server.py (ticket-state response only)'; AC1: 'show/MCP render attestations (kind -> record); omitted when empty'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `attestations: dict[str, dict] | None = None` to `TicketStateOut`, matching the reducer's 'omitted when empty' semantics (null → field absent in JSON via `model_dump(exclude_none=True)` or reducer-level omission).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one behavioral test (or CI assertion) that verifies (a) the `attestations` map renders correctly in `show`/MCP output when populated, (b) it is omitted when empty, and (c) no HMAC hex appears in the rendered output for any kind — so the security property in AC2 has an automated guard.
findings.finding: The plan introduces new user-facing rendering behavior (CLI show + MCP ticket-state response, including the security-sensitive 'no HMAC hex' property) but specifies no testing approach — not even a thin test reference, existing test pointer, or CI assertion mechanism. AC2 says 'verified here' but names no verification vehicle.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'Output carries NO HMAC hex for any kind (the strip itself lands in 114f; verified here).'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a test section or AC such as: 'A unit/integration test asserts that `show` output for a ticket with one attestation contains the kind key and no 64-char hex string; a separate assertion confirms the field is absent when `attestations` is empty. These tests are added to the existing `reads_cli` / `mcp_server` test files.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Label the `compute_validity` section in `docs/plan-review-gate.md` as a planned/future capability (shipping in 929e), so consumers don't attempt to call it at this story's shippoint.
findings.finding: The plan's AC3 says to document `compute_validity` in `docs/plan-review-gate.md`, but `compute_validity` does not exist anywhere in the current codebase and is scoped to a future story (929e per the 114f ticket description). Documenting an unimplemented function as if it is part of the current model risks misleading consumers about what is actually available at this story's shippoint.
findings.location: AC line: 'docs/plan-review-gate.md documents emission per kind, semantics, compute_validity, the legacy mirror'
findings.suggested_fix: Prefix the compute_validity documentation with: '(Planned — ships in 929e): `compute_validity` will be computed on read to determine attestation validity considering reopen events.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a stub spec for `compute_validity` (signature, return shape, per-kind dispatch contract) to either the plan body or the 114f/929e ticket so the `docs/plan-review-gate.md` section can document it accurately rather than speculatively.
findings.finding: `compute_validity` is referenced in AC3 as something `docs/plan-review-gate.md` must document ('immutable-record + `compute_validity` on read'), but `compute_validity` does not exist anywhere in the codebase (confirmed via grep). A documentation author has no source of truth for this function's signature, return type, or semantics to draw from — the documentation would be speculative.
findings.location: AC3: 'docs/plan-review-gate.md documents emission per kind, semantics, compute_validity, the legacy mirror + its deferred removal (352b)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add 929e to 'Depends on' (write the compute_validity doc section after 929e's interface is finalized) OR narrow AC3 to document only the conceptual 'validity-on-read' pattern and placeholder the compute_validity signature, leaving the exact API doc to 929e or a follow-up.
findings.finding: AC3 requires documenting `compute_validity` semantics (immutable-record + `compute_validity` on read) in docs/plan-review-gate.md, but `compute_validity` is defined in story 929e — which is currently `open`, not merged, and is NOT listed in this story's 'Depends on'. Documenting an API whose interface is not yet finalized risks the docs diverging from 929e's actual implementation (return shape, argument list, caller contract).
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC3's 'compute_validity' wording with: 'documents the validity-on-read model (attestation records are immutable; validity is computed by a caller-supplied verifier — `compute_validity`, defined in 929e — rather than stored); note the function signature as TBD pending 929e.' Alternatively, add 929e to Depends on and document the concrete signature once it merges.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion that covers the `CLAUDE.md` attestations note (e.g., 'CLAUDE.md contains a note describing the two-level attestations shape and pointing to `docs/plan-review-gate.md` for details').
findings.finding: The `CLAUDE.md` attestations note is listed as a Scope deliverable but has no covering acceptance criterion, making it an orphan that cannot be verified at review time.
findings.location: Scope section / Acceptance Criteria (all four ACs)
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: '`CLAUDE.md` contains a brief attestations note referencing the two-level `attestations.<kind>` shape and linking to `docs/plan-review-gate.md`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly decide and document whether `llm_format.py` is in scope for this story: either add an `attestations` compaction branch (mirroring `shorten_signature` logic per kind) or explicitly note it as a deferred follow-up alongside the 352b legacy-mirror removal.
findings.finding: The `to_llm()` function in `reducer/llm_format.py` (used by `show --output llm` and MCP list_tickets with llm format) has explicit per-field handling for `signature` (`shorten_signature`) but has no handler for `attestations`. After 114f, an `attestations` map will appear in the public state dict passed to `to_llm()`, and the per-kind records will pass through verbatim (uncompacted, using the full `attestations` key). This is not a hex-leak (public_state strips the hex before to_llm sees it), but it is an unacknowledged verbosity gap — the `signature` compaction rationale ('the FACT of a signature, not the signature itself') applies equally to each per-kind attestation record. The plan neither addresses `llm_format.py` nor acknowledges that it is intentionally out of scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the verification artifact for AC2 (e.g., 'add a unit/snapshot test asserting no 64-char hex string appears in show/MCP output for a fixture attestation record') so the assertion is auditable in-session.
findings.finding: AC2 says this story 'ASSERTS the rendered output carries no hex for any kind' but specifies no verification artifact or mechanism (unit test, snapshot, grep assertion, etc.). An implementer cannot know whether a manual inspection, a regex check in an existing test, or a new test case is expected.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'Output carries NO HMAC hex for any kind (the strip itself lands in 114f; verified here).'
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC2 to: 'Output carries NO HMAC hex for any kind; verified by a test (e.g., pytest snapshot or regex assertion) that checks rendered show/MCP output against a fixture attestation containing a known signature and confirms the hex is absent.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC4 by defining 'per-kind certified check' in the plan body (or replace with the exact documentation content/heading it requires, e.g., 'a section explaining how `verify-signature --kind K` serves as a per-kind CI check').
findings.finding: The phrase 'per-kind certified check' in AC4 does not appear anywhere in the plan body's What/Scope sections, creating a terminology mismatch where the AC's verify step references a concept not defined in the described work.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC4 vs. What section
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, add one sentence: 'Document the per-kind CI readiness pattern: a CI job calls `verify-signature --kind K` for each expected kind and treats a non-zero exit as a certification failure.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan what concrete artifact (code change vs. test assertion vs. both) is required in `reads_cli.py` and `mcp_server.py` — specifically, whether the `attestations` rendering is entirely passive (auto-flows from 114f's `public_state()` extension) and this story's work in those files is purely test/assertion coverage of that passthrough.
findings.finding: The plan lists `reads_cli.py`/show path and `mcp_server.py` (ticket-state response) as scope files requiring changes, but the actual rendering of `attestations` is passive: once 114f adds the hex-stripped `attestations` map to `public_state()`, it flows through `show_state() → public_state() → json.dumps()` and `TicketStateOut.model_validate()` (which uses `extra='allow'`) without any code change to either file. The plan never articulates what concrete change—if any—is actually needed in these files, leaving an implementer uncertain whether to add code or simply write a test asserting the passthrough works.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line to the Scope section: 'The `reads_cli.py` and `mcp_server.py` changes are test-only: `attestations` flows passively through the existing `public_state()` → `json.dumps` / `extra="allow"` pipeline; this story adds integration-level assertions (no hex in output, omitted when empty) rather than new rendering code.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC2's verification precondition: add an explicit statement that AC2 can only be certified after 114f's `public_state()` attestations branch (114f AC5) is confirmed merged, and name a cross-story integration test that exercises the strip end-to-end.
findings.finding: AC2 asserts 'Output carries NO HMAC hex for any kind (the strip itself lands in 114f; verified here)' — but the `public_state()` dedicated `attestations` branch that performs this strip (iterating per-kind records) is scoped to 114f's AC, not this story. If 114f ships without that branch, this story has no strip to verify and AC2 is unverifiable.
findings.location: AC line: 'Output carries NO HMAC hex for any kind (the strip itself lands in 114f; verified here)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: '(requires 114f AC5 merged; this story adds an integration test asserting `public_state(state)['attestations'][kind]` contains no `signature` key for any kind, covering the strip boundary)'

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or prose guidance) specifying the rendering behavior for malformed/partial attestation records and `compute_validity` errors in both `show` and the MCP ticket-state response (e.g., render a `[invalid: <reason>]` inline or skip the record with a warning).
findings.finding: The plan specifies the happy path (attestations rendered) and the empty case (omitted when empty), but does not address what the show/MCP output should look like when an attestation record is partially malformed, or when `compute_validity` raises an error during rendering — leaving the non-happy-path for the only two user-facing surfaces unspecified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC #1 / src/rebar/_engine_support/reads_cli.py show path / src/rebar/mcp_server.py
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the AC or Scope section: 'If an individual attestation record fails validation or compute_validity raises, the field is rendered with an inline error marker (e.g., `"<kind>": {"error": "invalid record"}`) rather than aborting the full show/MCP response.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
